issue: English uses the adjective/adverb form in "A little snail goes slow." For graded EFL, this models nonstandard grammar.
suggestion: Change to "A little snail goes slowly."

issue: "Slow snail says hello to all" is missing the article before a singular count noun and sounds unnatural.
suggestion: Change to "The slow snail says hello to everyone."

issue: Korean summary has an unnatural particle: "바닷속을 노는".
suggestion: Change to "파란 물고기가 보글보글 거품을 불며 바닷속에서 노는 이야기예요."

issue: "Pip came close, so his light helped the beetle" has an awkward causal link; coming close does not directly mean the light helped.
suggestion: Change to "Pip came close to help the beetle."

issue: Option "다른 핍의 친구" is unnatural and can be read as "another Pip's friend."
suggestion: Change the option to "핍의 다른 친구" or "다른 반딧불이 친구".

issue: "sings as one" is an idiomatic phrase that is too hard for A1 readers.
suggestion: Change to "Every night, all the pond friends sing together."

issue: "A bridge of friends" is poetic but awkward and may confuse A2 readers.
suggestion: Change to "A bridge made by friends is the best bridge."

issue: English says "Near the leaves," likely the strawberry plant leaves, but Korean says "나뭇잎," which means tree leaves.
suggestion: Change translation_ko to "딸기 잎 근처에서 작은 회색 깃털 하나가 눈에 띄었어요."

issue: "They knelt down because the first pool was very still" gives an odd cause-and-effect, and "knelt" is also difficult for A2.
suggestion: Change to "They sat beside the first pool. The water was very still."

issue: Korean "살아 있고, 어디서도 살 수 없으니까" is accurate but potentially confusing because "살" repeats with different meanings.
suggestion: Change to "살아 있고, 어디에서도 돈으로 살 수 없으니까."

issue: "It held the ladybug warm" is not natural English grammar/collocation.
suggestion: Change to "It kept the ladybug warm and stayed with the lonely bird."
